ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic submarine sandwich class (FOODON_00002473); no specific Italian BMT variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers sandwiches broadly; no Italian cold cut sub-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.description: 1 six-inch sandwich (ala carte, no drink or side)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard six-inch Subway Italian B.M.T. sandwich served ala carte, weighing approximately 233 g, comprising bread, cold cuts, and default salad vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sandwich — USDA Branded Food (FDC ID 2099464 / Subway Restaurants nutrient data)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA Branded Food entry for Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sandwich used as primary reference. Per-100g values derived by dividing reported per-serving values (serving size 233 g) by 2.33. Values reflect default preparation with standard vegetables and no added condiments beyond olive oil blend and vinegar. This is a commercial branded product; USDA Branded Food is the approved source for packaged/commercial products per Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread-based submarine sandwich
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Italian B.M.T. specifically. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a white bread submarine sandwich with mixed fillings (GI range 40–50 reported for similar cold cut subs). The Italian bread roll used by Subway is a refined wheat roll with moderate GI; the protein and fat content of the cold cuts further attenuate the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 64.1) / 100 ≈ 28.8, rounded to 28.7. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~1,587 mg per 6-inch serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the Subway Italian B.M.T. due to its very high sodium content (~1,587 mg per serving), which represents approximately 69–106% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 1,500–2,300 mg; choosing a lower-sodium filling and omitting added sauces is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~28.7 per serving) from refined wheat bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with the Italian B.M.T. due to its moderate-to-high glycemic load (~28.7 per 6-inch serving) driven by the refined wheat bread roll; opting for a wholegrain or wrap option and monitoring portion size can help attenuate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat from processed meats (salami, pepperoni, ham), and cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of the Italian B.M.T. because the combination of processed meats (Genoa salami, pepperoni, ham) contributes significant saturated fat (~6.1 g per serving), cholesterol (~70 mg per serving), and very high sodium (~1,587 mg per serving), all of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Italian B.M.T. (Bigger, Meatier, Tastier) is a commercially prepared cold cut submarine sandwich sold at Subway restaurants worldwide, including Singapore where it is available ala carte without a drink or side. The sandwich is built on a six-inch Italian white bread roll and layered with three types of cured and processed meats: Genoa salami, pepperoni, and ham. Default salad vegetables include lettuce, tomato, onion, green pepper, and cucumber, finished with an olive oil blend and vinegar. The bread is soft and slightly chewy, the meats are savory, salty, and mildly spiced, and the vegetables add freshness and crunch. Per 100 g, the sandwich provides approximately 218 kcal, 11.6 g protein, 7.7 g fat (2.6 g saturated), and 27.5 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notably high at approximately 681 mg per 100 g (~1,587 mg per 6-inch serving), primarily from the cured meats and bread. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low GI) due to the moderating effect of protein and fat from the cold cuts, but the glycemic load per serving is moderate-to-high (~28.7) because of the substantial carbohydrate portion from the bread roll. Clinically, the Italian B.M.T. is a concern for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sodium, saturated fat from processed meats, and refined carbohydrate content. It is not suitable for halal or kosher diets due to the pork-derived meats. In Singapore, it is sold at Subway outlets and listed on field survey delivery platforms.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from USDA Branded Food data for the Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sandwich, which is an approved source for commercial branded products. Per-100g values are calculated by dividing per-serving values by the reported serving weight (233 g). The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar cold cut submarine sandwiches, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g, derived from USDA Branded Food data for the Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sandwich (serving size 233 g). Values reflect default preparation with standard vegetables and olive oil blend/vinegar; additional condiments (mayonnaise, mustard, etc.) would alter the nutritional profile. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Food entry for this product. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Food entry for this product. 4. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 5. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 7. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 9. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 10. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 11. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 12. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 13. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 14. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 15. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 16. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 17. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 18. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 19. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 20. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 21. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source entry used. 22. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA Branded Food entry for this product. 23. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Vegetables', which is incorrect for this sandwich product; corrected to 'Mixed' reflecting the composite nature of the sandwich. 24. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for the Subway Italian B.M.T. is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database. 25. The Italian B.M.T. contains pork-derived meats (salami, pepperoni, ham) and is not halal or kosher. 26. Singapore field survey listing price: SGD 9.60 (ala carte). 27. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for a Western sandwich sold in Singapore context.
